data.narrative:
Milrem Robotics is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $30M+. The valuation analysis relies on a peer universe of 10 comparables: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms at matching stage and sector, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling uses the $30M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $57M. The peer-median method benchmarks against 6 same-stage, same-sector private comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B (median $1.2B), reflecting the broader market positioning of defense-tech companies at this funding stage.

These methods produce a wide but defensible range: $48M (floor, anchored to near-term capital needs), $628M (geometric midpoint), and $1.4B (ceiling, aligned with high-end peer valuations). The median of $628M represents the center of gravity given current information. Material uncertainty stems from the disparity between stage-progression and peer methods, reflecting ambiguity around Milrem's competitive positioning relative to higher-valued peers. Validation would require deeper analysis of revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and technology differentiation relative to the peer set.
